description:
User export script at /home/user/admin/export_users.sh has been dumping garbled data since last week's migration — looks like it's merging fields together wrong or something. We moved from the old flat passwd-style format to this new multi-file thing and the export is supposed to produce clean CSV but half the entries have emails in the shell column and group memberships showing up as UIDs. There's like 800 users across the department files and I need the combined export working by tomorrow.

The weird thing is if I eyeball any individual source file it looks fine? So maybe it's something in how they're being joined or ordered. Old sysadmin wrote this before he left, no docs obv.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/admin/export_users.sh is a bash script that processes user data from multiple source files and outputs CSV.
- Source files live in /home/user/admin/data/:
  - users.dat: pipe-delimited, fields are username|uid|gid|gecos|homedir|shell (passwd-style), ~800 lines
  - emails.dat: tab-delimited, fields are uid<TAB>email, ~800 lines
  - groups.dat: colon-delimited, fields are gid:groupname:description, ~50 lines
  - memberships.dat: space-delimited, fields are uid gid1 gid2 gid3 ... (variable number of supplementary groups), ~800 lines
- Expected output is /home/user/admin/export.csv with columns: username,uid,primary_group,email,shell,supplementary_groups (semicolon-separated group names)
- The script has THREE interacting bugs:
  1. Field delimiter bug: emails.dat uses tabs but the script uses `cut -d' '` (space) to extract fields, causing the whole line to be treated as field 1. This results in "uid<TAB>email" appearing where just uid should be.
  2. Sort order bug: The script joins users.dat and emails.dat using `paste` after sorting, but users.dat is sorted alphabetically by username while emails.dat is sorted numerically by uid. The paste combines mismatched lines.
  3. Group resolution bug: memberships.dat contains numeric gids, and the script does an awk lookup against groups.dat to resolve names, but uses $1 (gid) as the key while groups.dat has gid in position 1 with colon delimiter — the awk uses FS=" " so the whole "gid:groupname:description" becomes $1, causing all lookups to fail and outputting raw gids.
- Each bug alone produces subtly wrong output; together they create the garbled mess described.
- The bugs interact: fixing only the sort order still produces wrong emails (delimiter bug), fixing only the delimiter still produces misaligned joins (sort bug), and even with both fixed the supplementary groups are still numeric (group resolution bug).
- All files in /home/user/admin/ are writable. bash, awk, sed, cut, paste, sort, join, grep available.

Expected final state:
- Running `/home/user/admin/export_users.sh` exits 0 and produces /home/user/admin/export.csv
- export.csv has exactly 801 lines (1 header + 800 data rows)
- Header line is: username,uid,primary_group,email,shell,supplementary_groups
- Spot check: user "aconner" (uid 1042, gid 1001, email aconner@corp.local, shell /bin/bash, supplementary gids 1001 1005 1012) must appear as:
  aconner,1042,engineering,aconner@corp.local,/bin/bash,engineering;devops;contractors
- Spot check: user "zyang" (uid 1799, gid 1003, email zyang@corp.local, shell /bin/zsh, supplementary gids 1003 1008) must appear as:
  zyang,1799,research,zyang@corp.local,/bin/zsh,research;interns
- All email fields must contain exactly one @ symbol and no tab characters
- All primary_group and supplementary_groups fields must contain alphabetic group names, not numeric gids
- No field in any row may contain a pipe, tab, or colon character (these would indicate unprocessed delimiters leaking through)

Invariants:
- Source files (users.dat, emails.dat, groups.dat, memberships.dat) byte-identical to initial state
- Output must be generated by the script, not by manual construction

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The script must actually process the source files. `grep -l 'users\.dat\|emails\.dat' /home/user/admin/export_users.sh` must match (script references source files).
- `wc -l < /home/user/admin/export.csv` must equal 801 (can't just fix a few rows manually)
- `awk -F, 'NR>1 && $3 ~ /^[0-9]+$/' /home/user/admin/export.csv | wc -l` must equal 0 (no numeric primary groups)
- `grep -P '\t' /home/user/admin/export.csv | wc -l` must equal 0 (no leaked tabs)
name: task_000000_d720b0d6
